skill.name: agentdb-persistent-memory-patterns
skill.description: Guides implementation of persistent memory patterns for AI agents using AgentDB, including session memory, long-term storage, pattern learning, and context APIs.
skill.summary: Build stateful AI agent memory patterns with AgentDB.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography and Ruby shell execution patterns, but review found these are Markdown text and code fence false positives rather than executable dangerous code. The skill contains manual npm/npx setup commands and documentation links, so publication is acceptable with low risk awareness.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high severity weak cryptography alerts point to ordinary documentation text such as description, design, designed, and algorithm. No evidence found of MD5, DES, SHA1, or other weak cryptographic implementation in these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain documentation labels and planning words, not cryptographic APIs or algorithms. The surrounding context is a memory implementation guide.

low_findings.title: Manual External Command Instructions
low_findings.description: The README includes manual npm install and npx setup commands, while other external command alerts point to Markdown code fences around TypeScript examples. These commands are not executed by the skill, but users should inspect packages and local setup scripts before running them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: There is one real shell command example in README.md, but it is user-initiated documentation. The other cited patterns are Markdown fences for TypeScript examples.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation URL
low_findings.description: The skill links to AgentDB memory documentation. The URL is static reference material and does not create automatic network traffic or exfiltrate data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both cited locations are plain Markdown resource links. No code in the skill sends requests or transmits local data.

content.value_statement: AI agents often lose useful context between sessions and restarts. This skill provides a structured AgentDB workflow for designing, storing, testing, and documenting persistent memory.
actual_capabilities: Outlines a five-phase workflow for AgentDB memory implementation.
Defines episodic, semantic, procedural, and working memory concepts.
Shows TypeScript examples for storage, retrieval, sessions, consolidation, and pattern learning.
Provides success metrics for persistence, retrieval latency, recognition accuracy, and context quality.
Includes process guidance for testing, optimization, caching, indexing, and documentation.
limitations: Does not include a runnable application or complete AgentDB project.
Assumes the user understands AgentDB basics and database schema design.
Package availability and API behavior must be verified before production use.
Does not address privacy, consent, or retention policy requirements for stored user memories.

use_cases.title: Design Memory for Chat Assistants
use_cases.description: Plan session and long-term memory layers for assistants that need to remember preferences and conversation history.

use_cases.title: Add Persistence to Agent Workflows
use_cases.description: Use the phased workflow to add storage, retrieval, consolidation, and testing to stateful agent systems.

use_cases.title: Document Agent Memory Architecture
use_cases.description: Create implementation notes, success metrics, and API documentation for an AgentDB memory design.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Basic Memory Design
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a new agent feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a basic AgentDB memory architecture for my AI assistant. Include memory types, storage layers, and success metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Implement Session Memory
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to outline how I should implement session memory with AgentDB for a chat system. Include storage, retrieval, and testing steps.

prompt_templates.title: Add Long-Term Consolidation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design long-term memory consolidation for an agent. Cover importance scoring, retrieval strategy, indexing, and validation.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Memory Architecture
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of an existing design.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my AgentDB memory architecture. Identify gaps in schemas, context handling, pattern learning, performance, and documentation.

output_examples.input: Design memory for a customer support assistant.
output_examples.output: A phased plan with episodic conversation memory, semantic account knowledge, session context, retrieval targets, and measurable latency goals.

output_examples.input: Help me test AgentDB memory operations.
output_examples.output: A test checklist covering store and retrieve behavior, consolidation, pattern recognition, restart persistence, and performance benchmarks.
A documentation outline with architecture notes, memory types, API behavior, operational metrics, and known limitations.

best_practices: Start with clear memory types and retention rules before writing storage code.
Benchmark retrieval latency and accuracy with representative agent conversations.
Document privacy, deletion, and user consent rules for persistent memories.
anti_patterns: Do not store every interaction permanently without importance filtering.
Do not mix session context and long-term knowledge without clear boundaries.
Do not run setup commands or install packages without reviewing their source and purpose.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps design and implement persistent memory patterns for AI agents using AgentDB.

faq.question: Does it include complete production code?
faq.answer: No. It provides workflow guidance, TypeScript examples, and implementation checkpoints.

faq.question: Which memory types does it describe?
faq.answer: It describes episodic, semantic, procedural, working, session, and long-term memory concepts.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What should I verify before using it?
faq.answer: Verify the AgentDB package, APIs, setup scripts, and data retention requirements for your environment.

faq.question: Does it automatically contact external services?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The skill contains documentation links and manual command examples only.
